points.text: Kymera Therapeutics announced Gilead Sciences exercised its option to license KT-200, an oral CDK2 molecular glue degrader, for development in oncology indications.

Kymera Therapeutics presented Phase 1b BroADen trial data for KT-621, an oral STAT6 degrader, at the American Academy of Dermatology Annual Meeting, showing consistent biological and clinical activity in atopic dermatitis.
Kymera Therapeutics — KT-200 — Oncology (CDK2 target) — Gilead Sciences exercised option to license oral CDK2 molecular glue degrader; development candidate selected for advancement.
Kymera Therapeutics — KT-621 — Atopic Dermatitis — Phase 1b BroADen trial presented at AAD 2026; single-arm open-label study demonstrated consistent impact on biomarkers and clinical measures.

Kymera Therapeutics — Oral molecular glue degrader commercialization — KT-200 (CDK2) + KT-621 (STAT6) — Gilead option exercised for KT-200; Phase 2b BROADEN2 (AD) and BREADTH (asthma) trials ongoing with data expected mid-2027 and late-2027.

KT-200 — Mechanism: Oral CDK2 molecular glue degrader — Phase: Pre-clinical/Development candidate selection — Population: Oncology indications — Status: Licensed by Gilead Sciences via option exercise.

KT-621 — Mechanism: Oral STAT6 degrader (first-in-class) — Phase: 1b (BroADen) — Population: Atopic dermatitis — Comparator: None (single-arm) — Results: Consistent impact on biomarkers and clinical measures reported at AAD 2026.
Kymera Therapeutics / Gilead Sciences — Option exercise for KT-200 license — Oral CDK2 molecular glue degrader — Gilead exercised option to advance development candidate; financial terms undisclosed.
